name: Mailchimp
one_liner: Email marketing and automation platform whose Marketing API lets agents manage audiences, campaigns, automations, reports, and e-commerce data.
access_notes: A Mailchimp account (including the free plan) can generate an API key from account settings and call the Marketing API immediately; no human approval. API key is used as HTTP Basic auth (anystring:TOKEN) or OAuth2 bearer. What you can do depends on plan level, but working credentials are self-serve. Transactional (Mandrill) requires a separate transactional account/add-on.
api_notes: Marketing API (REST v3.0) is broad: audiences/lists, members, campaigns, automations, templates, reports, segments, tags, e-commerce stores/orders/products (100+ operations). Base URL https://<dc>.api.mailchimp.com/3.0/. Separate Transactional API (formerly Mandrill) for event-driven email/SMS, and Open Commerce (GraphQL, open source). Rate limit: 10 simultaneous connections, 120s timeout.
mcp_notes: Mailchimp ships an official MCP server for its Transactional Messaging service at https://mandrillapp.com/mcp (bearer API key auth; send email/SMS, templates, diagnostics). No official MCP found for the core Marketing API, but multiple community MCP servers exist (e.g. damientilman/mailchimp-mcp-server ~112 tools, Zapier, Pipedream, Apify).
main_blocker: None significant; API is public with self-serve credentials. Available operations scale with account plan level, and data-center prefix must be derived from the key.

evidence.claim: Marketing API supports API key (HTTP Basic) and OAuth2 bearer auth; base URL https://<dc>.api.mailchimp.com/3.0/; 10 simultaneous connection limit
Mailchimp offers Marketing API, Transactional (Mandrill), and Open Commerce (open-source, GraphQL) developer products
Official Mailchimp Transactional Messaging MCP server hosted at https://mandrillapp.com/mcp using bearer API key auth

open_questions: Whether the free plan permits generating an API key is stated as plan-dependent in docs (free is generally sufficient for API access, but capabilities vary by tier).
